name: faster_rcnn

members.name: FasterRCNNConfig
members.signature: FasterRCNNConfig(num_classes: int = 91, in_channels: int = 3, backbone_layers: tuple[int, int, int, int] = (3, 4, 6, 3), backbone_bn_eps: float = 0.0, fpn_out_channels: int = 256, rpn_anchor_sizes: tuple[int, ...] = (32, 64, 128, 256, 512), rpn_anchor_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 1.0, 2.0), rpn_pre_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_post_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_nms_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_min_size: float = 0.001, rpn_score_thresh: float = 0.0, rpn_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.3, roi_size: int = 7, roi_sampling_ratio: int = 2, roi_representation_size: int = 1024, roi_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, roi_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, bbox_reg_weights: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 5.0), canonical_scale: int = 224, canonical_level: int = 4, score_thresh: float = 0.05, nms_thresh: float = 0.5, max_detections: int = 100)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 91, in_channels: int = 3, backbone_layers: tuple[int, int, int, int] = (3, 4, 6, 3), backbone_bn_eps: float = 0.0, fpn_out_channels: int = 256, rpn_anchor_sizes: tuple[int, ...] = (32, 64, 128, 256, 512), rpn_anchor_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 1.0, 2.0), rpn_pre_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_post_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_nms_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_min_size: float = 0.001, rpn_score_thresh: float = 0.0, rpn_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.3, roi_size: int = 7, roi_sampling_ratio: int = 2, roi_representation_size: int = 1024, roi_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, roi_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, bbox_reg_weights: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 5.0), canonical_scale: int = 224, canonical_level: int = 4, score_thresh: float = 0.05, nms_thresh: float = 0.5, max_detections: int = 100)

members.extended:
Faster R-CNN (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015) replaces external proposal methods
(selective search) with a learned Region Proposal Network (RPN) that shares
convolutional features with the detection head.  This makes the entire
pipeline end-to-end trainable and near real-time.

Architecture overview (ResNet-50-FPN variant):
  Image → ResNet-50 backbone (frozen BN, C2-C5) → FPN (P2-P5 + pool)
  FPN levels → RPN head → region proposals (per-level top-k + NMS)
  FPN levels + proposals → MultiScale RoI Align (7×7) → TwoMLPHead
    (fc6/fc7) → FastRCNNPredictor (cls + per-class bbox)

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description: Object classes incl. background (91 for COCO).

parameters.name: rpn_anchor_sizes
parameters.description: One anchor scale per FPN level (5 levels).

parameters.name: rpn_pre_nms_top_n
parameters.description: Proposals kept per level before NMS (test 1000).

parameters.name: rpn_post_nms_top_n
parameters.description: Proposals kept per image after NMS (test 1000).

parameters.name: rpn_bg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Anchor→GT IoU upper bound for background.

parameters.name: roi_bg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Proposal→GT IoU upper bound for bg.

members.name: FasterRCNNForObjectDetection

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, proposals: list[Tensor] | None = None)
methods.summary: Run Faster R-CNN on a (pre-processed) image batch.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: (B, C, H, W) resized + normalised image batch.

parameters.name: proposals
parameters.description:
Optional precomputed per-image proposals.  When
       `None` the RPN generates them.

returns.description:
`ObjectDetectionOutput` with raw RoI-head outputs:
`logits`     : (Σ proposals, num_classes) class logits.
`pred_boxes` : (Σ proposals, num_classes, 4) per-class boxes.
`proposals`  : per-image proposal tensors.

methods.name: postprocess
methods.signature: postprocess(output: ObjectDetectionOutput, image_sizes: list[tuple[int, int]] | None = None, proposals: list[Tensor] | None = None)
methods.summary: Softmax → per-class score filter → per-class NMS → top-k.
methods.extended:
Mirrors the reference `postprocess_detections`: drops the
background class (slot 0), score-thresholds, removes empty boxes,
runs per-class NMS, and keeps the top `max_detections` scores.

parameters.name: image_sizes
parameters.description:
Unused (boxes are already clipped); accepted for API symmetry
with other detectors.

returns.description:
One dict per image with `"boxes"` `(D, 4)`, `"scores"`
`(D,)`, `"labels"` `(D,)` int64.

members.summary: Faster R-CNN with a ResNet-50-FPN backbone (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015).
members.extended:
The two-stage anchor-based detector in its modern reference
configuration: a ResNet-50 trunk with frozen batch-norm feeds a
Feature Pyramid Network, a Region Proposal Network emits per-image
proposals from five pyramid levels, and a Fast R-CNN-style RoI head
classifies + refines RoI-aligned crops.  The submodule layout mirrors
the reference detector so the COCO `box AP 37.0` checkpoint loads
strict and reproduces inference.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use the `faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn`
factory for the COCO-pretrained configuration (`num_classes = 91`).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.faster_rcnn import faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn
>>> model = faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn()
>>> model.eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[-1]   # num_classes
91
>>> dets = model.postprocess(out, image_sizes=[(224, 224)])
>>> sorted(dets[0].keys())
['boxes', 'labels', 'scores']
notes:
See Ren et al., "Faster R-CNN: Towards Real-Time Object Detection with
Region Proposal Networks", NeurIPS 2015 ([arXiv:1506.01497](https://arxiv.org/abs/1506.01497)) and Lin et
al., "Feature Pyramid Networks for Object Detection", CVPR 2017.  The
model expects an already resized + normalised image batch; final
detections come from `postprocess`.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: backbone
attributes.description:
ResNet-50 `body` + `fpn` producing five feature maps
`[P2, P3, P4, P5, pool]` at strides `4/8/16/32/64`.

attributes.name: rpn
attributes.description: Proposal head shared across all pyramid levels.

members.name: faster_rcnn
members.signature: faster_rcnn(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: FasterRCNNResNet50FPNWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Faster R-CNN with a ResNet-50-FPN backbone (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015).
members.extended:
Alias of `faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn` — the canonical reference
configuration: a ResNet-50 trunk (frozen BN) feeding a Feature Pyramid
Network, a Region Proposal Network over five pyramid levels, and a Fast
R-CNN RoI head.  The COCO config uses `num_classes = 91`.  Reaches
COCO box AP of 37.0 (reference `fasterrcnn_resnet50_fpn`).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` →
the `DEFAULT` tag (`FasterRCNNResNet50FPNWeights.COCO_V1`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

returns.description:
Detector with the ResNet-50-FPN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.faster_rcnn import faster_rcnn
>>> model = faster_rcnn(num_classes=91)
>>> model.eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[-1]
91

members.name: faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn
members.signature: faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: FasterRCNNResNet50FPNWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Faster R-CNN with a ResNet-50-FPN backbone (COCO-pretrained).
members.extended:
Builds the reference `fasterrcnn_resnet50_fpn` detector: ResNet-50
backbone with frozen batch-norm, a 5-level Feature Pyramid Network
(P2-P5 + a parameter-free pool level for RPN), anchors at sizes
`((32,),(64,),(128,),(256,),(512,))` and ratios `(0.5, 1.0, 2.0)`,
and a Fast R-CNN RoI head (TwoMLPHead + FastRCNNPredictor) over
MultiScale RoI Align.  COCO box AP 37.0, ~41.8M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` →
the `DEFAULT` tag (`FasterRCNNResNet50FPNWeights.COCO_V1`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

returns.description:
Detector with the ResNet-50-FPN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.faster_rcnn import faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn
>>> model = faster_rcnn_resnet50_fpn()
>>> model.eval()
>>> out = model(lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224))
>>> out.logits.shape[-1]
91
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from the reference
`FasterRCNN_ResNet50_FPN_Weights.COCO_V1` checkpoint and hosted under
`lucid-dl/faster-rcnn-resnet-50-fpn`.  The detector expects an
already resized + normalised image batch (the
`lucid.utils.transforms.Detection` preset).

members.name: FasterRCNNResNet50FPNWeights
members.extended:
Single COCO checkpoint converted from the reference
`FasterRCNN_ResNet50_FPN_Weights.COCO_V1` (91 classes, box AP 37.0).

summary: Faster R-CNN — object detection (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015).
extended:
Paper: "Faster R-CNN: Towards Real-Time Object Detection with
        Region Proposal Networks"

ResNet-50-FPN reference configuration (Lin et al., CVPR 2017 FPN) with a
COCO-pretrained checkpoint.
citation: Ren, Shaoqing, et al. "Faster R-CNN: Towards Real-Time Object Detection with Region Proposal Networks." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2015.
theory:
Faster R-CNN removes the last hand-engineered component of the
R-CNN family — the external selective-search proposal step — by
introducing a **Region Proposal Network (RPN)** that *shares
convolutional features* with the detection head.

The RPN is a small fully-convolutional network that slides over
the backbone feature map.  At every spatial position it evaluates
$k$ reference boxes called **anchors** (the paper uses
$k = 9$ from 3 scales × 3 aspect ratios) and emits two
heads:

- **Objectness** — a $2k$-dim softmax classifying each
  anchor as foreground or background.
- **Box regression** — a $4k$-dim parameterised offset
  $t = (t_x, t_y, t_w, t_h)$ refining anchors toward
  ground-truth boxes.

Anchors with IoU $\ge 0.7$ against any GT are positive;
$\le 0.3$ are negative.  The multi-task loss is



$$
L(\{p_i\}, \{t_i\}) =
    \tfrac{1}{N_{\mathrm{cls}}} \sum_i L_{\mathrm{cls}}(p_i, p_i^*)
    + \lambda \, \tfrac{1}{N_{\mathrm{reg}}} \sum_i p_i^*
      \, L_{\mathrm{reg}}(t_i, t_i^*).
$$

Top-scoring proposals (after NMS) replace selective search
completely.  The **same backbone** then feeds the Fast R-CNN
head, so the entire detector is end-to-end trainable.  With a
VGG-16 trunk the whole pipeline runs at ≈5 fps while matching
or exceeding selective-search Fast R-CNN accuracy — a milestone
that established two-stage anchor-based detection as the
dominant paradigm and seeded all later FPN / Mask R-CNN / Cascade
R-CNN extensions.
